generatedNote: Canonical tag-schema source of truth. Both packages/core/src/lib/schemas/content-types.ts (TAG_TO_CONTENT_TYPE) and apps/desktop/src-tauri/src/commands/vault.rs (tag_map) derive from / are asserted against this file. Do not hand-edit downstream mappings; edit here.

canvas.description: An Excalidraw scene / freeform visual canvas. Content ground truth (a body with Excalidraw elements) overrides tags.

code.description: A source-code note rendered with the code editor (CodeMirror).

spreadsheet.description: Tabular CSV data rendered as a grid.

task.description: Action items, commitments, and work to be done. Lifecycle: todo → in-progress → done (or blocked/cancelled). Link tasks to the project and person they relate to.

due.description: ISO-8601 date/datetime the task is due, or '' for none

completed.description: ISO-8601 date the task was completed (set when status=done)

context.description: Where the task came from / supporting notes

briefing.description: A daily briefing generated by the intelligence system. One per day at vault/agent/briefings/{date}.

subject.description: Who or what the briefing covers

dashboard.description: A project or topic dashboard aggregating key views and metrics

project-page.description: A public-facing project page or landing page

meeting.description: A meeting note (often paired with a transcript). Links to attendee person notes and project notes. status tracks processing; event_status tracks the calendar lifecycle.

source.description: Where the meeting note originated (fathom, meetily, calendar, manual)

recording_id.description: Recording ID from the source platform

fathom_url.description: Fathom share URL, when source is fathom

event_status.description: Calendar lifecycle (for events synced from Google Calendar)

transcript.description: A meeting transcript from Fathom or Meetily. Process by extracting attendees, action items, and decisions, then add the 'processed' tag.

source_id.description: Recording/meeting ID from the source platform

synced_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp the transcript was pulled into the vault

concept.description: An idea, framework, theory, or domain concept

source.description: Where this concept was first captured

writing.description: An essay, article, blog post, or published piece

research.description: A research note, investigation, or literature review

proposal.description: A formal proposal for a project, grant, or initiative

questionnaire.description: A questionnaire or set of discovery questions (document subtype).

spec.description: A technical specification or design document

script.description: A speaker script, talk outline, or presentation narrative

person.description: A person Benjamin interacts with. Store contact info, role, and relationship context. Link to their organizations and projects.

projects.description: Projects this person is involved in

last-contact.description: ISO-8601 date of last interaction (canonical name is hyphenated)

source.description: Where this entity was first captured

organization.description: An organization, company, DAO, nonprofit, or collective

people.description: Key people associated with this org

projects.description: Projects run by or associated with this org

source.description: Where this entity was first captured

decision-record.description: A record of a decision made, with context and alternatives

grant-application.description: A grant application or fellowship submission

project-update.description: A periodic status update for a project

report.description: A generated or written report summarizing work or processing results

processed-by.description: Who or what generated this report

page.description: A standalone page — about, colophon, or other informational content

index.description: An index page aggregating related notes by topic or project

project.description: A project Benjamin is involved in. Contains goals, status, collaborators, and links to related meetings and tasks.

org.description: Parent organization (wikilink string; canonical key is 'org')

source.description: Where this entity was first captured

keywords.description: Comma-separated keywords for matching (e.g. 'bioregional, food chain, regenerative agriculture')

agent-insight.description: Intelligence analysis output. Patterns, escalations, and opportunities detected by the intelligence scan.

agent-skill.description: A scheduled agent skill. The skill scheduler reads these notes and dispatches Claude Code CLI with the note content as the prompt.

runAtHour.description: Hour of day to run (0-23, local time)

dependsOn.description: skillName that must complete before this skill runs

capture.description: Notes captured directly by the user (text or voice).

email.description: An email thread synced from Gmail. Classified with importance tags (urgent, action-required, informational).

isUnread.description: Whether the thread has unread messages

message-thread.description: A chat conversation from WhatsApp, Telegram, or other bridged platform. Classify with importance tags.

governance-config.description: Commons governance constitution (singleton). Holds the master enabled switch (the one-way bootstrap lock), the bootstrap owner, the amend/disable threshold policy id, and system defaults. Once `enabled` is true, this note can only be changed through an amend_governance proposal.

enabled.description: The bootstrap lock. false = owner configures freely; true = governance is self-amending and all governance-* mutations require governance. One-way in practice: flipping back to false itself requires an amend_governance proposal.

bootstrap_owner.description: The genesis admin subject (email) who seeds roles/policies before enablement.

amend_policy.description: Note id of the governance-policy governing the amend_governance action (changing/disabling governance itself). The constitutional threshold — should be strictly harder than ordinary content policies.

default_threshold_n.description: Fallback approvals required when no more-specific policy matches an action.

default_eligible_role.description: Fallback role whose members may approve when no policy matches.

governance-role.description: A commons governance role: a named bundle of powers, optionally scoped to a tag (nested/tag-scoped governance, e.g. gardener of #watershed). Powers ∈ review, publish, certify_gardener, manage_policy, arbitrate, invite, revoke, amend_governance.

name.description: Role name (e.g. gardener, steward, admin, arbiter).

scope_type.description: Whether the role acts globally or only within a tag.

scope.description: The tag this role is scoped to when scope_type=tag (empty for global).

governance-membership.description: Binds a subject (email) to a governance-role. Optional expiry gives term limits / periodic recall for free.

role.description: Note id or name of the governance-role.

granted_by.description: Who granted this membership (subject or proposal id).

expires_at.description: ISO-8601 expiry, or empty for no expiry.

governance-policy.description: A collective-choice rule: how many sign-offs, from which role, over what window, are required for an action on a scope, and whether approval auto-publishes. The HOW-MANY axis.

action.description: The governed action (e.g. edit_note, new_entry, publish, certify_gardener, add_admin, remove_admin, amend_governance).

scope.description: The tag or note id the policy applies to (empty for global).

quorum.description: Minimum participation (absolute count) required for the vote to count; 0 = no quorum.

eligible_role.description: Role whose members' approvals count toward the threshold.

window_seconds.description: Time window within which approvals must accrue; 0 = no window.

auto_publish.description: Whether meeting the threshold also publishes (moves the live pointer) vs. only approves.

governance-proposal.description: A pending change awaiting sign-off: a content revision, a new entry, a role/membership change, a policy change, or an amend_governance action. Evaluated against its matching governance-policy.

action.description: The action being proposed (matches a governance-policy action).

target.description: The resource the proposal acts on (note id, tag, role id, or 'governance-config').

payload.description: The proposed change (revision id, JSON patch, or role/policy body).

opened_at.description: ISO-8601 open time (drives the policy window).

governance-vote.description: One sign-off on a governance-proposal by an eligible member. Distinct (voter, proposal) pairs are what the threshold counts.

governance-audit.description: An append-only audit entry recording a governance mutation (grant/role/policy change, approval, publish, sanction). The commons's legible memory.

before.description: JSON of prior state (empty for creations).

entity.description: Supertype for actors/beings in the commons (person, organization, ecological-entity). REA's 'Agent' is a role an entity may also play — the neutral supertype lets ecological entities exist for SENSING reasons, not only economic ones. Query all actors via tag:entity; narrow via entity_kind or the subtype tag.

entity_kind.description: The is-a subtype axis (works today; parent_names is a later seed enhancement).

sensing_or_responding.description: The cleavage gate: does this serve SENSing state/threats, RESPONDing for resilience, or BOTH? A note that can't declare one is out of scope.

wikidata_qid.description: Stable global identifier (Wikidata QID) — survives renames; links to the global graph.

same_as.description: External authority URLs/ids this note mirrors (GBIF, Murmurations, USGS, GeoNames).

indigenous_governed.description: Sovereignty membrane: when true the note is EXCLUDED from public publishing and federation (TK/BC-labeled, partner-controlled). Never index indigenous knowledge; partner by pointer.

ecological-entity.description: A located living/geophysical being or feature (a river, creek, forest stand, wetland, observed population). Carries GeoJSON geometry. The SENSING substrate — matters even when no economic event touches it.

species.description: Wikilink to the species note, when this entity is a population of one.

geometry.description: GeoJSON geometry (Point/LineString/Polygon/MultiPolygon), WGS84, [lon,lat]. RFC 7946 — no crs member.

geo.description: Simple centroid { lat, lon } for quick lookups.

sensing_or_responding.description: The cleavage gate: does this serve SENSing state/threats, RESPONDing for resilience, or BOTH? A note that can't declare one is out of scope.

wikidata_qid.description: Stable global identifier (Wikidata QID) — survives renames; links to the global graph.

indigenous_governed.description: Sovereignty membrane: when true the note is EXCLUDED from public publishing and federation (TK/BC-labeled, partner-controlled). Never index indigenous knowledge; partner by pointer.

species.description: A taxon (knowledge-level). Field names are the literal Darwin Core terms so notes round-trip to GBIF/OBIS; anchored to stable GBIF/iNat ids. Distinct from ecological-entity (a located occurrence/population).

gbifTaxonKey.description: GBIF backbone usageKey — the stable species id (re-fetch handle).

indigenous_governed.description: Sovereignty membrane: when true the note is EXCLUDED from public publishing and federation (TK/BC-labeled, partner-controlled). Never index indigenous knowledge; partner by pointer.

place.description: A bounded area whose state we sense or within which we respond (schema.org/Place + GeoCoordinates). Subtypes via place_kind; watersheds have their own tag for HUC nesting.

geometry.description: GeoJSON geometry (Point/LineString/Polygon/MultiPolygon), WGS84, [lon,lat]. RFC 7946 — no crs member.

geo.description: Simple centroid { lat, lon } for quick lookups.

sensing_or_responding.description: The cleavage gate: does this serve SENSing state/threats, RESPONDing for resilience, or BOTH? A note that can't declare one is out of scope.

same_as.description: External authority URLs/ids this note mirrors (GBIF, Murmurations, USGS, GeoNames).

indigenous_governed.description: Sovereignty membrane: when true the note is EXCLUDED from public publishing and federation (TK/BC-labeled, partner-controlled). Never index indigenous knowledge; partner by pointer.

watershed.description: A hydrologic unit, keyed by USGS HUC. The HUC code encodes its own nesting (HUC2⊃HUC4⊃…⊃HUC12), so the place hierarchy is derivable by prefix — no join.

huc12.description: 12-digit Hydrologic Unit Code — the stable id.

parentHuc.description: Parent HUC (this code truncated) — the nesting pointer.

bbox.description: Derived bbox for indexed spatial pre-filtering.

sensing_or_responding.description: The cleavage gate: does this serve SENSing state/threats, RESPONDing for resilience, or BOTH? A note that can't declare one is out of scope.

wikidata_qid.description: Stable global identifier (Wikidata QID) — survives renames; links to the global graph.

event.description: A dated occurrence registering a change of state or a coordinated response (REA Economic Event / schema.org/Event).

sensing_or_responding.description: The cleavage gate: does this serve SENSing state/threats, RESPONDing for resilience, or BOTH? A note that can't declare one is out of scope.

resource.description: A stock/flow regenerative response can produce, consume, or steward (REA/Valueflows Economic Resource). Light REA for v1.

conformsTo.description: Wikilink to a resource specification (VF conformsTo).

sensing_or_responding.description: The cleavage gate: does this serve SENSing state/threats, RESPONDing for resilience, or BOTH? A note that can't declare one is out of scope.

indigenous_governed.description: Sovereignty membrane: when true the note is EXCLUDED from public publishing and federation (TK/BC-labeled, partner-controlled). Never index indigenous knowledge; partner by pointer.

signal.description: The cybernetic core: a sensed condition that DEMANDS a response for resilience — a policy/corporate/ecological threat or signal. No general ontology models this because none is purpose-bound to resilience; it is the raison d'être of the commons.

response.description: Wikilinks to response recipe/flow notes (close the sense→respond loop).

sensing_or_responding.description: The cleavage gate: does this serve SENSing state/threats, RESPONDing for resilience, or BOTH? A note that can't declare one is out of scope.

wikidata_qid.description: Stable global identifier (Wikidata QID) — survives renames; links to the global graph.

same_as.description: External authority URLs/ids this note mirrors (GBIF, Murmurations, USGS, GeoNames).

indigenous_governed.description: Sovereignty membrane: when true the note is EXCLUDED from public publishing and federation (TK/BC-labeled, partner-controlled). Never index indigenous knowledge; partner by pointer.

recipe.description: A reusable how-to for RESPONDING — a social or material process others can re-run. OpenCivics Protocol (objective/roles/steps) on the inside, schema.org/HowTo on the wire; unifies VF Recipe (material) and OpenCivics Protocol (social).

bundles.description: Wikilinks to sub-recipes/protocols this playbook composes.

sensing_or_responding.description: The cleavage gate: does this serve SENSing state/threats, RESPONDing for resilience, or BOTH? A note that can't declare one is out of scope.

wikidata_qid.description: Stable global identifier (Wikidata QID) — survives renames; links to the global graph.

same_as.description: External authority URLs/ids this note mirrors (GBIF, Murmurations, USGS, GeoNames).

indigenous_governed.description: Sovereignty membrane: when true the note is EXCLUDED from public publishing and federation (TK/BC-labeled, partner-controlled). Never index indigenous knowledge; partner by pointer.

flow.description: Links entities/resources into a planned or observed action — who did/will do what to which resource (REA duality / VF Commitment/Event). Light REA: the small connective layer, not full process accounting.

at.description: ISO-8601 time of the event (or due, for a commitment).

planned.description: True for a commitment (planned), false for an observed event.

sensing_or_responding.description: The cleavage gate: does this serve SENSing state/threats, RESPONDing for resilience, or BOTH? A note that can't declare one is out of scope.

indigenous_governed.description: Sovereignty membrane: when true the note is EXCLUDED from public publishing and federation (TK/BC-labeled, partner-controlled). Never index indigenous knowledge; partner by pointer.

herbal-use.description: The join between a species and a medicinal/ethnobotanical use (USDA Dr. Duke's, CC0). One species carries many uses; each use cites its authority. Where species and earth-systems knowledge intersect — the resilience case for bioregional medicine.

sensing_or_responding.description: The cleavage gate: does this serve SENSing state/threats, RESPONDing for resilience, or BOTH? A note that can't declare one is out of scope.

indigenous_governed.description: Sovereignty membrane: when true the note is EXCLUDED from public publishing and federation (TK/BC-labeled, partner-controlled). Never index indigenous knowledge; partner by pointer.

governance-revision.description: A content revision snapshot (approval≠publishing, Plan 1 G4). The revision note CONTENT is the snapshot; metadata links it to the target note, its parent revision, and the proposal that produced it. published=false means approved/staged but not live.

note.description: Target note id (empty while a staged new_entry has not been created yet).

parent.description: Previous revision note id (the history chain).

proposal.description: The governance-proposal that produced this revision.

origin.description: How this revision came to be.

published.description: Whether this revision is (was) the live/published content.

payload.description: For staged new_entry: JSON {path,tags,metadata} needed to create the note on publish.

drift.note: Rust tag_map maps 'dashboard' to 'project'; TS TAG_TO_CONTENT_TYPE maps it to 'dashboard'. Direct per-tag contentType disagreement.

Same contentType, but ORDER drift: Rust places 'project' at priority index 7 (just after 'dashboard'), while TS places it LAST (lowest priority). Consequence: a note tagged 'project' plus any document-subtype tag (meeting, transcript, concept, writing, research, proposal, spec, script, person, organization, decision-record, grant-application, project-update, report, page, index) OR 'project-page' resolves to 'project' under Rust but to that subtype (document / website) under TS. Fix by moving 'project' last in the Rust tag_map to match TS first-match-wins.
